summary: assertcoreutils typosquats chai and executes attacker-fetched code on require()
details: Package impersonates the chai assertion library (identical lib/ tree, README and homepage references to chaijs) but ships a remote-code dropper. The declared main entry index.js unconditionally spawns a detached background node process against lib/chai/utils/addAssertion.js every time any consumer require()s or imports the package (child_process.spawn with detached:true, stdio:'ignore', unref()). lib/chai/utils/addAssertion.js is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io string-array rotation, IIFE checksum, hex-mangled identifiers, base64/percent-decode wrapper) to hide an https.get to an attacker-controlled URL; the fetched response body is passed to new Function('require', body)(require), granting attacker-controlled code full Node module privileges with the real require bound. The combination of typosquat naming against a top-tier package, library-load auto-execution, obfuscated downloader, and new Function(remote body)(require) is an unambiguous install/load-time RCE dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
